title: Wall Street Shifts from Crypto Pilots to Ethereum Adoption, Says Expert
summary: Wall Street is moving beyond initial crypto experiments and diving deeper into Ethereum, according to Vivek Raman, cofounder of Etherealize. The infrastructure is in place, but widespread adoption hasn't yet boosted ETH's value as expected.
body:
Wall Street institutions are transitioning from small-scale crypto experiments to more significant investments in Ethereum, according to Vivek Raman, cofounder of Etherealize. In an interview with CoinDesk, Raman noted that while the infrastructure for Ethereum has been largely built, the scale of adoption hasn't yet translated into higher prices for ETH.

Raman emphasized that the current phase is one of transition, where the foundational work is complete, but the market is still waiting for mainstream adoption to drive up the value of Ethereum. He highlighted that Wall Street is moving past the pilot phase and is now more seriously exploring the potential of Ethereum for various financial applications.

This shift could be significant for everyday users as it may lead to more stable and widely accepted use cases for Ethereum. As institutional investors become more involved, the ecosystem could see increased stability and innovation, potentially making Ethereum more accessible and useful for everyday transactions and applications.

For those interested in the crypto space, this is a time to watch how Wall Street's deeper involvement in Ethereum plays out. Keep an eye on any major announcements from financial institutions regarding their Ethereum strategies, as these could signal broader market trends.
